skill.name: creating-feedback-loops
skill.description: Creates structured feedback loops that help Claude, Codex, and Claude Code review work, track recurring issues, and improve responses over time.
skill.summary: Build structured review, learning, and improvement loops for AI-assisted work.
skill.author: C0ntr0lledCha0s

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown code fences as Ruby shell backtick execution and matched weak-crypto or reconnaissance keywords inside ordinary prose. Manual review found no executable code, prompt injection text, network behavior, secret access, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Misread as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings point to Markdown fenced examples and templates, not Ruby backtick execution or runnable shell commands. The skill content provides review workflows and checklist formats only.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown fences or nearby documentation blocks in SKILL.md. No executable script, command invocation, or user-controlled shell interpolation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Match in Prose
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm findings occur in natural-language text such as the frontmatter description and feedback-loop instructions. No cryptographic algorithm, hash function, or security implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain ordinary words and instructions about design, analysis, documentation, and feedback. I found no MD5, SHA-1, DES usage, cryptographic code, or related implementation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Keyword Match in Review Questions
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance findings are normal checklist and template wording. They do not instruct the assistant to inspect host identity, enumerate systems, collect environment data, or bypass permissions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are benign quality-review prompts and learning-log headings. No commands such as whoami, id, uname, or filesystem enumeration appear in context.

content.value_statement: AI-assisted work can repeat mistakes when review habits are informal. This skill provides structured loops for self-review, user feedback, checkpoints, and measurable improvement.
actual_capabilities: Defines immediate, interactive, checkpoint, and pattern-learning feedback loops.
Provides templates for baseline metrics, issue logs, quality gates, and learning logs.
Guides self-review before delivery for correctness, completeness, clarity, security, and efficiency.
Helps identify recurring issues and convert them into prevention strategies.
Supports measurable improvement tracking with quantitative and qualitative metrics.
limitations: Does not execute automation or collect metrics by itself.
Requires users or agents to record feedback consistently.
Does not replace domain-specific testing, security review, or expert validation.
Long-term improvement depends on access to prior notes or session history.

use_cases.title: Improve Repeated Coding Work
use_cases.description: Set checkpoints after each file, test run, and final review to catch recurring code quality issues.

use_cases.title: Refine Team Review Standards
use_cases.description: Create shared quality gates and feedback templates for more consistent AI-generated deliverables.

use_cases.title: Track Writing Quality Over Time
use_cases.description: Use issue logs and learning points to reduce repeated clarity, completeness, and tone problems.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Simple Feedback Loop
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a simple feedback loop for this task. Include review steps, what to track, and when to ask me for feedback.

prompt_templates.title: Add Quality Checkpoints
prompt_templates.prompt: Design checkpoints for this project. Show what should be reviewed at each milestone and how corrections should be applied.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Recurring Issues
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these recurring issues and turn them into a pattern-learning loop with root causes, prevention steps, and metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Full Improvement System
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete continuous improvement system for my AI workflow, including baseline metrics, feedback collection, quality gates, learning logs, and verification.

output_examples.input: Create a review process for AI-assisted code changes.
output_examples.output: A milestone-based checkpoint plan with review criteria for requirements, tests, edge cases, security, and final delivery readiness.

output_examples.input: Help me stop repeating the same documentation mistakes.
output_examples.output: A pattern log that groups recurring clarity issues, identifies root causes, and turns each issue into a prevention checklist.

output_examples.input: Measure whether my AI workflow is improving.
output_examples.output: A compact scorecard with baseline metrics, current results, improvement percentages, unresolved issues, and next focus areas.

best_practices: Define the feedback loop before starting complex work.
Track a small set of measurable quality signals consistently.
Convert recurring issues into specific prevention steps.
anti_patterns: Recording feedback without changing the workflow.
Adding so many checkpoints that work slows down without clear benefit.
Treating self-review as a replacement for tests or expert review.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create review loops, quality checkpoints, issue logs, learning logs, and measurable improvement workflows.

faq.question: Does it automatically remember feedback across sessions?
faq.answer: No. Long-term tracking requires saved notes, project files, or another memory system.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow is tool-agnostic and works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is this only for coding tasks?
faq.answer: No. It can support coding, writing, documentation, planning, and other repeatable work.

faq.question: Does it run commands or install software?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill is documentation-only and does not contain executable scripts.

faq.question: How should teams use it?
faq.answer: Teams should agree on quality gates, track recurring issues, and review improvement metrics at regular milestones.
